def: s.m. One who rears pigeons; one who keeps sporting pigeons, a pigeon-fancier; (met.) a gallant

One who rears pigeons and bets upon their flight; a pigeon-fancier; pigeon-flyer. kabūtar-bā/?/ī , n. f. Rearing of pigeons; pigeonflying. kabūtar pakaṛnā, yā marnā , v. a. To catch pigeons. kabūtar-khānā , n. m. A dovecote; dove house. Kabūtar-khāne kā sā hāl hai, ek āyā ek chalā . The world's a dovecote; one enters, another goes out. kabūtar kā phūl , n. The snowy, but inodorous blossom of the Nagamallie. kabūtar kā jhāṛ. Justicia nasuta. kabūtar kī jaṛ . The Nagamallic root.(?) kabūtar laṛānā , v. a. To fight pigeons. janglī kabūtar , A wild pigeon.

s. m. A pigeon, dove. کبوتر باز kabūtar-bāz, One who rears pigeons. کبوتر بچه kabūtar-bacha, s. m. 1. A young pigeon. 2. An unripe poppy-head, wrapped up in peasemeal and fried in butter or oil. کبوتر خانه kabūtar-ḵẖāna, s. m. Dovecot, dovehouse.
subs: s.m. A young pigeon; — an unripe poppy-head, wrapped up in peasemeal and fried in butter or oil
The snow-white (but inodorous) blossom of the Naga-mallie, Justicia nasuta

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَبُوتَر (kabūtar, “pigeon; dove”).

def: s.f. A female pigeon; — (met.) a village, or rustic, dancing-girl.
more: n. f. 1. A she-pigeon. 2. A village dancing woman. ☞ So called in ridicule of her style of dancing in the course of which she draws up the ends of her skirt like a fantail pigeon. Her style is, however, natural and graceful, which is more than can be said of the generally ugly contortions of the artificially taught Nautch women of towns.

ety: inf. n. of قبض 'to take, seize,' &c.
def: s.f. Seizing, catching; seizure, capture; taking possession of; possession; confiscation, sequestration; — contraction; astringency; constipation, costiveness; — a receipt, voucher; — tax, tribute

A receipt; an aquittance roll; acknowledgment. qabz bil-jabr . Forcible possession. qabz karne-vālī, qābiz ; H aṛ karne-vālī , An astringent. qabz o dakhl , Complete or full possession. qabz-vār . In the way of seizure or distraint.

(Heb. To meet together, to contract) s. f. 1. Contraction. 2. Costiveness. 3. A receipt. 4. Tax, tribute. قبض الوصول ḳabẓu-l-wuṣūl, s. f. A receipt, acknowledgment. قبض کرنا ḳabẓ karnā, To seize, take possession of. قبض هونا ḳabẓ honā, To be seized, &c.

v.t. To catch, seize; to take possession of; to contract, &c.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْض (qabz), from Arabic قَبْض (qabḍ). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi قبض (qbẓ /⁠qabz⁠/).

To reinstate. qabzah pānā , v. n. To enter on possession ( dakhl pānā , 2). qabzah paṭṭī-dārī . Land held in severalty. qabzah-i-judāgānah, yā alahdah . Separate possession; land held in severalty; an independent holding. qabzah-i-chand-rozah . Temporary tenure or possession. qabzah-i-hīn hayātī . A life tenure. qabza-dārī , n. f. Occupancy. qabzah dilānā , v. a. To put in possession. qabzah denā , v. a. To make over charge; to give delivery. qabzah rakhnā , v. a. 1. To keep; retain; hold; to be in possession of; enjoy; to hav…
(from قبض) s. m. 1. Grasp, gripe (of the hand), clutch; possession. 2. Handle, hilt. قبضه کرنا ḳabẓa karnā, To seize, to take possession of.

more: n. f. S. कुच Pālī, kucho . The breasts of a girl on the verge of puberty. 1. Woh kanchan sī uskī kucheṅ lāl lāl, Bharī raṅg se qumqume kī misāl . Mir Hasan. So fair her breasts, with so ruddy a tinge, As the liquid with which you fill a syringe. 2. Raṅg bhabūkā, peṭ mulāyam, aur kuchoṅ meṅ sakhtī Sīne se le nāf talak ek sandal kī sī takhtī hai . Jurat. Her face was fair, her body soft, firm set her bubbies stood, From chest to navel smooth she was as plank of sandal wood.

def: s.f. A crowd, a dense mass; — adj. Crowded, thick, dense, closely packed, or planted, &c., stuffed together ( = gać-pać).

s.m. corr. of kać-bać, q.v. s.m. pl. See s.v. kać. s.m. Glass; crystal; an ornament of crystal; quartz; alkaline ashes; any salt of potash or soda in a glassy or crystalline state; a class of diseases of the eye (as gutta serena, &c.)
more: n. f. lit . splash, squash. Mud, mire, etc, so called from the sound.
1. s. f. Crowd. 2. adj. Close, thick, stuffed together: see گچپچ gach-pach. ¶ kich-pich, s. f. Mud, mire, &c.
subs: s.m. Black salt, a medicinal salt (prepared by calcining fossil salt and the fruit of the emblic myrobalan together; it consists chiefly of muriate of soda, with a small quantity of iron, lime, and sulphur, and is a tonic aperient)

def: s.m. lit. 'Crowded together'; the Pleiades.
s.m. = kaććāpan, q.v. s.m. Unripeness, rawness, greenness; crudity, immaturity; inexperience, simplicity; inferiority, &c. (see kaććā).

more: n. m. 1. Unripe melon. Ham chhoṛī nimāj bandgi, ab khet pe kachrā khaibo! Farrukh. I have done with prayer and kneeling, Now I'll take to melon-stealing. 2. A pod of cotton. Bāṛ meṅ bāṛ, bāṛ meṅ kachrā, kachre ne dī bahū, bahū ne dīyā susrā . A riddle. Ans. Rui binaulā . In the field a cotton plant, and in the plant a pod soon grew, The pod raw cotton (a daughter-in-law) gave, from the cotton cotton seeds (a mother-in-law) anew. 3. A belly like a pumpkin; a pot-belly.
s. m. 1. The raw خربوزه ḵẖarbūza, q. v. 2. Clay.

s. m. A kind of pickle. کچومرکر ڐالنا kachūmar kar ḍālnā, To cut to pieces.
subs: or kaćūmar nikālnā or nikāl-ḍālnā (-kā), 'To slice up for pickling,' to cut to pieces, to make mince-meat (of), to beat to a pulp.

ety: v.n. fr. قدم, inf. n. qadm, 'going before, preceding'
def: s.m. The foot; sole of the foot; a foot's length; a footstep, step, pace; — a going before; merit
more: n. m. 1. Foot. 2. A pace; step; footstep. Qadam-i-darveshāṅ radd-i-balā . P. Prov. Where holy Dervis' footsteps lead the front of evil must recede. qadam uṭhā-kar chalnā , v. n. To walk fast. qadam-bāz , adj. Ambling. Baṛā qadam-bāz ghoṛā hai. qadam ba-qadam chalnā , v. a. To walk in one's footsteps. qadam baṛhānā, yā āge rakhnā . See baṛhnā , 9, 12, āge baṛhnā , 1, 2, and pāoṅ baṛhānā . 1. To take long steps; step out; walk fast. 2. To trench upon; exceed; pass bounds; encroach; transgress. qadam-bos h. , v. a. 1. To kiss the feet, as a token of respect. 2. To make obeisance; pay one's respects. qadam-bosī , n. f. 1. Kissing the feet of a superior. 2. Obeisance; homage; genuflexion; respectful salutation. qadam chūmnā, yā lenā , v. a. To kiss one's feet reverentially ( qadam-bos h .). qadam chhūnā, yā lenā, qadam ko hāth lagānā , To touch or clasp one's feet reverentially. Qadam ko hāth…

(Heb. and Ch. To go before) s. m. 1. A pace, a step, a footstep. 2. The foot, the sole of the foot. 3. The going before, merit. قدم باز ḳadam-bāz, Fleet. قدم بقدم ḳadam-ba-ḳadam, Step by step, gently, by degrees. قدم بوس ḳadam-bos, 1. adj. Kissing the feet, shewing respect, paying respects. 2. s. or قد مبوسي ḳadam-bos-ī, s. f. Kissing the feet, obeisance, respects. قدم چومنا ḳadam chūmnā, 1. To kiss the feet. 2. To leave the company of another, to bid adieu. قدم چهـوني ḳadam chhūne, To touch or embrace oneʼs feet. قدم رنجه فرمانا ḳadam ranja farmānā or قدم رنجه کرنا ḳadam ranja karnā, To take the trouble of going or coming (spoken respectfully). قدم لگنا ḳadam lagnā, 1. To take protection. 2. To walk well (a horse). قدم ليني ḳadam-lene, To acknowledge the superiority of another (either in good or evil qualities; but most frequently in the last). قدم وار ḳadam-wār, adj. Capable of being t…
subs: To walk fast, to step out
v.n. To put the foot well forward, to step out, to take long steps, to walk fast; to put the foot (beyond, -se); to go or step beyond bounds, to exceed due limits, to transgress; to encroach (on)
adv. Step with step; in the footsteps (of); step by step, slowly, gently, by degrees
adj. Kissing the feet (of, -ke), showing respect (to); paying (one's) respects; making obeisance; — qadam-bos honā (-ke), To kiss the feet (of), &c.

v.n. To take the trouble of going or coming (to, — spoken respectfully)
adv. Step by step; at a walking or an easy pace
(with loc.), To plant the foot firmly (on); to establish or secure (one's) footing

def: adj. & adv. Ancient, old, antiquated, immemorial; archaic; — s.m. An old inhabitant, &c. ( = qadīmī)
more: adj. قدم going before. 1. Old; ancient; immemorial. Khudā-band ham kadim ke rahatvān, yoh kāl kā basā oṛ saë . Rus. 2. Antiquated; archaic. 3. Ancestral; hereditary. qadīm, qadīm-ul-aiyām se , adv. Of old; from ancient times; long ago; a long time ago; in the olden time; in days of yore; from time immemorial. qadīm raiyat . An occupant of land by old hereditary descent. qadīm naukar, yā namck-khuār . An old servant. qadīm'ī, qadīm , n. 1. An old inhabitant of a village. 2. def: (called qāf-ě-qarashat), the twenty-seventh letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twenty-fourth of the Persian, and twenty-first of the Arabic, alphabet). It is a strongly articulated guttural, uttered by pressing the root of the tongue on the throat, and has been compared in sound to the caw of a raven. It does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analogous letter in Sanskrit. As a numeral qāf denotes 100.
more: called qāf qarshat , the 21st letter of the Arabic Alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analogous letter in Sanskrit. In abjad , it is 100.

the twenty-first letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analogous letter in Sanskrit. Its sound differs from that of ک or the English k, in the utterance of it by pressing the root of the tongue on the throat; and, has been compared to the cawing of a raven. In the Arabic notation of numbers by the letters of the alphabet it stands for 100. It is a radical letter. It is sometimes used for غ as آقا for آغا; for ک as قشط for کشط To excoriate; and in words adopted from Persian, for ه as فستق from پسته A pistachio.

r: kāf, kāf-ě-tāzī, kāf-ě-ʻarabī, gāf, kāf-ě-ʻajamī, kāfʼ, ka, k, ka-kār
ety: called kāf-ě-tāzī, or kāf-ě-ʻarabī, 'the Arabic kāf,' to distinguish it from گ gāf, called kāf-ě-ʻajamī, 'the Persian kāfʼ
def: s.m. The letter or sound ka
more: the 22nd letter of the Arabic, 25th of the Persian, and 28th of the Hindustānī alphabet, corresponds to क in Sanskrit. Its sound is that of the English k . In abjad q. v. it stands for 20. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with ch , q. v. as kīk=chīk scream; parkānā= parchānā tame; sok=soch consideration; karek= kirach a particle: with p , q. v. as khisalnā=phisalnā to slip; dhokā=dhopā deceit; Pr. khoḍo=phoḍo a boil; bilaknā=bilapnā cry: with t , q. v. as chhoklā=chhotrā peel: with ṭ as chipaknā=chipaṭnā stick; bhukkū=bhuṭṭū a blockhead; jhapākā= jhapātā a rush; jhokā=jhotā a nod: with g as kirnā=girnā fall; S. kapol =H. gāl cheeks; S. shakaṭ =E. H. saggaṛ cart; bikasnā=bigasnā open; S. shāk =H. sāg potherb: with l as P. koch = P. loch squint-eyed; ūparkā=ūparlā upper; P. tāvak = P. tāval heifer: with d as P. kamar =H. & Panj. damar waist; khurkhurā=khurdarā rough; …

the twenty-second letter of the Arabic and twenty-fifth of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to क in Sanskrit. Its sound is that of the English k. To represent its Sanskrit aspirate ख ( kh ) in the Arabic characters, کهـ is adopted. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters, ک stands for twenty. Its permutations are, 1st, into خ as شاماخچه or شاماکچه A womanʼs bodice: 2d, into غ as کژگاو or غژگاو The bull of Tartary (Bos grunniens): 3d, Arabistically, into ق as منجنيق for منجنيک A warlike engine. In Persian, it is sometimes prefixed to a word, but more commonly it occurs disjoined; in which latter case it has the ه maktūbī or muḵẖtafī annexed to it, thus که, and being pronounced with kasr (ِ) it may be used in the following senses. 1. (کاف تعليل or assigning cause) Because, for. 2. (کاف بيان or کاف صله the relative pronoun) Who, which. 3. (کاف معرفه) conj. That. 4. (کاف عطف) And. …
subs: or ka-varg, s.m. (in Hindī Gram.), The consonants of the क ka, or guttural class (viz. क, ख, ग, घ, ङ), the guttural consonants collectively.

def: s.f. Foam; scum, &c. ( = kaf, q.v.). (called qāf-ě-qarashat), the twenty-seventh letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twenty-fourth of the Persian, and twenty-first of the Arabic, alphabet). It is a strongly articulated guttural, uttered by pressing the root of the tongue on the throat, and has been compared in sound to the caw of a raven. It does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analogous letter in Sanskrit. As a numeral qāf denotes 100.

def: pron. (old H. & Braj) What? which? how? why? (i.q. kyā, q.v.). pron. (obl. kāhe), What? how? why? Whether, or; — intj. What! how!
